LLC 5670 W Flamingo Rd, Ste D, Las Vegas NV 89103 Ph: (702) 720-7929 I Fax: (702) 342-8398 Form Date: 3/31/26 Referral Source Information: Referring Agency: Phone: Physician Name: Gary morris Patient Information: Patient Name: Leon Coburn Phone: 702)460-4006 Patient Address: Primary Diagnosis: osteoantritis Bilat Showlders Primary Needs for Home Health (check all that apply): Skilled Nursing Medication Management IV Antibiotics Wound Care Skilled Monitoring Diabetic Care Others: Physical Therapy Occupational Therapy Social Worker Home Health Aide Other Pertinent Information: GaiT training Gn G MD Signature Please FAX this form to (702)342-8398 Further Instructions: When we call, we will also need to obtain the following information before seeing the patient. If you have these available, feel free to fax along with this form. - Patient demographic information (including insurance info) - Recent H&P or recent progress notes - Medication List THANK YOU FOR YOUR REFERRAL!!! Yee Advanced Orthopedics & Sports Appointment: No upcoming appointment Medicine, PLLC Patient Information Name: LEON COBURN Home Phone: (702) 460-4006 D.O.B: 12/04/1941 Work Phone: Sex: Male Mobile Phone: (702) 460-4006 SSN: Email: KARI.COBURN@UNLV.EDU MRN: 267096 Preferred Contact Method: Phone PMS ID: 267096 Language: English Marital Status: Married Emergency Contact: Kari Coburn Race: White Emergency Contact Phone: 702-460-4006 Ethnicity: Not Hispanic or Latino Employer: Retired Professor Address: 7020 W DEER SPRINGS WY LAS VEGAS, Primary Care Provider: VARRAS, JOHN NV 891313427 Referring Providers: NG, JEFFREY Primary Insurance Information Carrier: Medicare of Nevada Policy #: Address: 8E90CT5FF02 PO BOX 6776 FARGO, ND 581086776 Group ID/Name: Phone Number: Secondary Insurance Information Carrier: BCBS Policy #: Address: PO BOX 60007 LOS ANGELES, CA YFZ695A76218 900600007 Group ID/Name: NVSUPWP0 Phone Number: Pharmacy Information Name: VONS #29-2613 Phone Number: 7025151821 Address: 6450 SKY POINTE DR LAS VEGAS, NV 89131 Fax Number: 7025151839 Printed on 3/31/26. 10:23 AM - Tracev ADVANCED COBURN, LEON Visit Note - March 19, 2026 PMS ID: Sex: DOB: Phone: MRN: 267096 Male 12/04/1941 (702) 460-4006 267096 Alerts blood thinners Chief Complaints: No defibrillator and no pacemaker. 1. F/U Shoulder Arthritis, Bilateral evaluated on March 16, 2026 Allergies Reviewed March 19, 2026. HPI: This is an 84 year old male who: No known drug allergies 1. presents with bilateral shoulder arthritis, effusion. Patient is post left shoulder cortisone injection. Patient reports Medications some improvement since last office visit. Patient would like to discuss possible treatment options for the right shoulder. Patient is currently taking Tylenol for pain as needed. Reviewed and no changes noted March 19, 2026. amlodipine 10 mg Oral tablet atorvastatin 20 mg Oral tablet Vitals: Eliquis 5 mg Oral tablet escitalopram oxalate 5 mg Oral Date Taken By B.P. Pulse Resp. O2 Sat. Temp. Ht. Wt. BMI BSA tablet Arellano, Sophia 03/19/26 66.0 in* losartan 100 mg Oral tablet 160.0 25.8 1.8 10:52 lbs* FiO2 Medical History . Patient Reported Reviewed and no changes noted March 19, 2026. Exam: Arthritis Depressive disorder H/O: hypertension: Treated with meds Hypercholesterolemia: Treated with meds Exam Other: Atrial fibrillation Bilateral Shoulders Musculoskeletal History Reviewed and no changes noted Inspection: No erythema or swelling. No gross deformity. March 19, 2026. Adhesive capsulitis of shoulder: Both Palpation: Tenderness over glenohumeral joint lines bilaterally. left and right Chronic low back pain: Compressedspine H/O: rheumatoid arthritis Range of Motion: Impingement syndrome of shoulder region Right: Forward flexion 0-140°, abduction 0-120°, external rotation 0-60°. Left: Forward flexion 0-140°, abduction 0-120°, external rotation 0-60°. Musculoskeletal Family History Pain and crepitus with active and passive ROM. Diabetes Hypertension Strength: 5/5 rotator cuff strength with pain-limited effort. Other: stroke Special Tests: Neer and Hawkins mildly positive secondary to degenerative changes. No instability. Musculoskeletal Surgery Neurovascular: Distally neurovascularly intact. History of repair of musculotendinous cuff of shoulder: 2002-2008 Social History Impression/Plan: Reviewed and no changes noted 1. Shoulder Arthritis, Bilateral March 19, 2026. Primary osteoarthritis, right shoulder (M19.011) Single Question Alcohol Screening: 0 days Primary osteoarthritis, left shoulder (M19,012) Caffeine Use: Once a day located on the right shoulder joint. Exercise: Once a day Associated diagnosis: Shoulder Pain EtOH none Smoking status Former smoker Plan: Subacromial Steroid Bursal Injection. Start Smoking: 03/16/1956 Quit Smoking: 09/16/1984 The initial decision to perform this procedure was made after evaluation during this visit. Packs per day: 1 Years smoking: 27 The risks, benefits and alternatives of bursal injection were discussed with the patient. Risks include infection, bleeding, transient hyperglycemia, cartilage damage, and tissue atrophy at injection site. No certain guarantees have been made, patients understand that responses can vary and multiple procedures may be necessary. The Shoe Size patient was identified and timeout confirmed the correct site for the procedure. The patient was positioned Shoe size: 9.5 appropriately. The overlying skin was prepped with alcohol and Betadine Ultrasound guidance was performed at 5 Mhz with a linear probe. The use of direct ultrasound visualizations of the needle (rather than a palpation-guided procedure) was required to ensure accurate needle and injection placement, in order for diagnostic specificity when evaluating effectiveness of the injection, and for safety purposes to minimize risk of bleeding or injury to nearby neurovascular structures. The sterile ultrasonic gel was applied, and the probe was placed on the affected shoulder. The subacromial bursal space was visualized and the needle was introduced into the space. A total of 1 injections on the right shoulder joint were treated with 2 ml of Triamcinolone acetonide, 40mg. Megan Bichsel (Primary Provider) (Bill Under) WARM SPRINGS Page 1 (702) 740-5327 Work 7195 Advanced Way Las Vegas, NV 89113-3691 ADVANCED COBURN, LEON Visit Note - March 19, 2026 PMS ID: Sex: DOB: Phone: MRN: 267096 Male 12/04/1941 (702) 460-4006 267096 Tobacco Use Anesthesia was acheived with 2 ml of 1% lidocaine without epinephrine. Does not use vaping products Does not use smokeless tobacco Patient can resume activities as tolerated. Please contact the office if there is swelling, redness or pain at the injection site. Plan: Separate and Identifiable Documentation. Plan: Counseling - Shoulder DJD. Musculoskeletal care: I advised the patient that most patients with shoulder arthritis can achieve some measure of pain relief from nonsurgical options like medications, lifestyle modifications, exercise and physical therapy, and use of supportive devices. Surgery is reserved for patients who have persistent symptoms despite adequate conservative management Expectations: I counseled the patient about the natural history of shoulder arthritis which typically has exacerbations and remissions. Most people report pain accompanied by loss of range of motion and occasionally popping or clicking. Many people report that changes in the weather also affect the degree of pain from arthritis. The shoulder joint does not heal on its own when there is arthritis. The rate of progression of arthritis varies from person to person. With time, the symptoms of arthritis may get worse. For some, conservative management is not satisfactory. These individuals should consider surgical options. Contact office if shoulder pain worsens or if the joint becomes warm and accompanied by a fever. Plan: Prescription Medication Management. OTC Medication: Recommend over the counter medication PRN Plan: Recommendations. Impression: Bilateral shoulder arthritis, left shoulder effusion Diagnosis discussed at length with patient all questions were answered. Right shoulder injected in the office today. Patient will follow up in 3 months. Patient will call or return with any questions. Staff: Megan Bichsel (Primary Provider) (Bill Under) Sophia Arellano Electronically Signed By: Megan Bichsel, 03/19/2026 07:39 PM PDT Megan Bichsel (Primary Provider) (Bill Under) WARM SPRINGS (702) 740-5327 Work Page 2 7195 Advanced Way Las Vegas, NV 89113-3691
